panels.description: Composite score from CO2, PM2.5, TVOC, humidity, and temperature comfort.

targets.expr: clamp_min(100 - clamp_max(clamp_min((airgradient_co2_ppm{device=~"${device:regex}", location=~"${location:regex}"} - 600) / 18, 0), 35) - clamp_max(clamp_min((airgradient_pm2d5_ugm3{device=~"${device:regex}", location=~"${location:regex}"} - 5) * 1.2, 0), 30) - clamp_max(clamp_min((airgradient_tvoc_index{device=~"${device:regex}", location=~"${location:regex}"} - 100) / 10, 0), 15) - clamp_max(clamp_min((airgradient_humidity_percent{device=~"${device:regex}", location=~"${location:regex}"} - 60) * 1.5, 0), 10) - clamp_max(clamp_min((abs(airgradient_temperature_celsius{device=~"${device:regex}", location=~"${location:regex}"} - 22) - 2) * 2, 0), 10), 0)

panels.title: Indoor Air Score

panels.description: Linear projection 30 minutes ahead from the latest 30 minutes.
panels.title: CO2 Projection

panels.description: Positive values mean CO2 is rising over the last 30 minutes.
panels.title: CO2 Momentum

panels.description: Percentage of selected time range above 1000 ppm.
panels.title: High CO2 Share

panels.description: Percentage of selected time range above 12 ug/m3.
panels.title: High PM2.5 Share

targets.expr: clamp_min(100 - clamp_max(clamp_min((airgradient_co2_ppm{device=~"${device:regex}", location=~"${location:regex}"} - 600) / 18, 0), 35) - clamp_max(clamp_min((airgradient_pm2d5_ugm3{device=~"${device:regex}", location=~"${location:regex}"} - 5) * 1.2, 0), 30) - clamp_max(clamp_min((airgradient_tvoc_index{device=~"${device:regex}", location=~"${location:regex}"} - 100) / 10, 0), 15) - clamp_max(clamp_min((airgradient_humidity_percent{device=~"${device:regex}", location=~"${location:regex}"} - 60) * 1.5, 0), 10) - clamp_max(clamp_min((abs(airgradient_temperature_celsius{device=~"${device:regex}", location=~"${location:regex}"} - 22) - 2) * 2, 0), 10), 0)

panels.description: Normal, elevated, and high periods across the selected time range.
panels.title: Indoor Condition Timeline

panels.description: PM2.5 is scaled by 40 so it can be compared visually with CO2 on this exposure panel.
panels.title: Range Average vs Peak Exposure

panels.description: Serial number, device type, and firmware library labels from airgradient_info.
panels.title: Device Metadata
